title: MULTI-CLARID (Multimodal Category Learning and Resting-state Imaging Data)
description: MULTI-CLARID is a multimodal neuroimaging dataset combining simultaneous fMRI and EEG recordings from participants performing an auditory category learning task and resting-state fMRI. The dataset includes 63-channel EEG (plus ECG) with physiological monitoring (EOG, facial EMG, skin conductance), structural MRI (T1-weighted and PD-weighted UTE), and behavioral measures. This resource enables investigation of neural mechanisms underlying category learning and brain functional organization at rest.
methods_description: Data were acquired using a 3 Tesla Philips Achieva dStream MRI scanner with 32-channel head coil. EEG was recorded at 5 kHz using BrainAmp MRplus amplifiers (64 channels including ECG), with additional physiological monitoring via BrainAmp ExG MR amplifiers for EOG, EMG, and skin conductance. Auditory stimuli were presented via MR-compatible headphones. The category learning task comprised 180 trials with frequency-modulated sounds varying across five binary features (duration, frequency modulation direction, intensity, speed, and frequency range). Participants indicated category membership via button press with trial-by-trial feedback. Resting-state fMRI was acquired for 10 minutes with eyes closed. EEG preprocessing included MR-artifact correction, bandpass filtering (0.3-125 Hz), downsampling to 500 Hz, and pulse/motion artifact correction.
